Multi-agent
development
toolkit.

Specialized agents that route intelligently, share conventions, and deliver across 4 platforms — installed with a single CLI command.

CLI Commands
The Problem

Development friction, systematically eliminated.

⇄
Before

Context switching mid-task

→
After

Smart routing that detects intent, platform, and context — no config, no flags needed

◈
Before

Forgotten conventions

→
After

65+ skills loaded on-demand per task — patterns always available without bloating context

◉
Before

Manual quality checks

→
After

Two-stage review gates: code reviewer then a11y specialist catch issues before merge

◎
Before

Platform context loss

→
After

4-platform support — same workflow, correct conventions for web, iOS, Android, backend

Workflow

How it works.

01

Type naturally

Say what you mean: "fix the login bug", "plan the notification feature", "commit and push". No flags, no setup, no mode switching.

"fix the login page" "add dark mode" "plan search refactor"
02

Kit routes

Intent detection reads your prompt, checks git state, detects platform from file extensions, and routes to the right specialist automatically.

.tsx → web git staged → review active plan → build
03

Agent delivers

The matched agent loads its platform skills, applies your project conventions, and executes — reporting back to you when done.

code + tests audit report PR opened
Agent Roster

15 specialists. One orchestrated system.

Each agent is scope-bounded, skill-loaded, and ready to be dispatched. The main conversation is always the orchestrator.

⚙
Fullstack Developer

Implements features across web, iOS, Android, and backend

epost-fullstack-developer
◈
Planner

Creates phased implementation plans with TODO tracking

epost-planner
◉
Code Reviewer

Quality assurance, security audits, best practices enforcement

epost-code-reviewer
◎
Debugger

Root cause analysis and runtime error resolution

epost-debugger
◇
Tester

Comprehensive test suites, coverage analysis, validation

epost-tester
◑
MUJI

Design system, Figma-to-code, UI/UX, landing page craft

epost-muji
◐
A11y Specialist

WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across iOS, Android, and Web

epost-a11y-specialist
◫
Docs Manager

Documentation lifecycle, KB structure, orphan detection

epost-docs-manager
◰
Researcher

Technology research, best practices, comparative analysis

epost-researcher
◳
Git Manager

Commit, push, PR automation with security scanning

epost-git-manager
◬
Project Manager

Progress tracking, roadmaps, task routing, milestones

epost-project-manager
◭
Brainstormer

Creative ideation, problem-solving, architecture decisions

epost-brainstormer
◮
Kit Designer

Creates and maintains agents, skills, commands, hooks

epost-kit-designer
◯
MCP Manager

MCP server integrations, tool discovery, RAG queries

epost-mcp-manager
◴
Journal Writer

Development journals, decision logs, failure post-mortems

epost-journal-writer
Platform Coverage

Four platforms. Same discipline.

Platform detected from file extensions. Correct skills loaded. No configuration needed.

◈

Web

  • · Next.js 14 App Router
  • · React 18
  • · TypeScript 5+
  • · Tailwind CSS
  • · shadcn/ui + klara-theme
  • · Jest + Playwright
◉

iOS

  • · Swift 6
  • · SwiftUI + UIKit
  • · iOS 18+
  • · XCTest + XCUITest
  • · XcodeBuildMCP
◎

Android

  • · Kotlin
  • · Jetpack Compose
  • · MVVM + Hilt DI
  • · Room + Retrofit
  • · JUnit + Espresso
◇

Backend

  • · Java 8
  • · Jakarta EE 8
  • · WildFly 26.1
  • · Hibernate + PostgreSQL
  • · Maven + SonarQube
CLI

One CLI to ship the whole system.

epost-kit manages installation, profiles, packages, and updates. Works with any AI coding assistant — configure once, benefit everywhere.

Setup
$ epost-kit new

Scaffold a new project with agents and skills

$ epost-kit init

Install the kit into an existing project

$ epost-kit doctor

Validate installation health and fix issues

Packages
$ epost-kit profile list

Browse available configuration profiles

$ epost-kit package add web

Add platform-specific agent packs

$ epost-kit package remove android

Remove packages you don't need

Maintenance
$ epost-kit update

Update to the latest kit version

$ epost-kit lint

Check skills and agents for issues

$ epost-kit verify

Run full pre-release validation

Profiles — preconfigured agent bundles
fullall platforms + a11y + design
web-onlyNext.js + React + testing
mobileiOS + Android + a11y
minimalcore agents only
Packages — composable capability packs
coreorchestration + routing
platform-webNext.js, React, testing skills
platform-iosSwift, SwiftUI, Xcode skills
platform-androidKotlin, Compose, Gradle skills
a11yWCAG 2.1 AA across platforms
design-systemFigma, tokens, UI library
Developer Experience

Say it. It ships.

Three real interactions. No slash commands memorized. No mode switching. Just intent.

terminal

> fix the login page

✓ Detected: web (.tsx), Fix intent
→ Dispatching epost-debugger...
✓ Root cause: session token not refreshed on redirect
✓ Applied fix + added test case
→ epost-code-reviewer: LGTM
terminal

> plan the notification feature

✓ Detected: multi-platform, Plan intent
→ Dispatching epost-planner...
✓ Phase 1: Push service + backend endpoints
✓ Phase 2: iOS + Android foreground handling
✓ Phase 3: Web notification center UI
terminal

> commit and push

✓ Dispatching epost-git-manager...
✓ Staged: 4 files, +127 -23
✓ Commit: "feat(auth): refresh token on redirect"
✓ Push: origin/feat/auth-fix-token-refresh
✓ PR opened: #284
Engineering Leadership

The case for your team.

Not a productivity toy. A systematic approach to raising the floor for every developer on every platform.

◈

Velocity

Boilerplate handled. Developers unblocked.

  • · Agents handle routing, staging, commits, and PRs
  • · Parallel plan execution across 3+ independent tasks
  • · Platform detection means no context-setting overhead
↑ feature throughput
◉

Quality

Two-stage review gates on every change.

  • · Code reviewer: security, performance, correctness
  • · A11y specialist: WCAG 2.1 AA across all platforms
  • · Known-findings database prevents regressions
↓ bug escape rate
◎

Consistency

Shared conventions across every platform.

  • · 65+ skills encode your team's patterns
  • · Skill-discovery loads the right rules per task
  • · No "works on my platform" drift
= consistent standards
Automation

End-to-end workflows.

Chain agents into pipelines that automate entire development processes — from idea to shipped PR.

◉

Automated Code Audit

Every PR audited for security, quality, a11y, and performance
◈

PO-to-Prototype

Feature description → working branch with PR in ~20 min
◭

Brainstorming

Ideas → evaluated options → validated spike branch
◬

Dev Dashboard

Automatic progress tracking from agent outputs — zero reporting
⇄

Cross-Branch Fixes

Fix once, propagate to all affected branches automatically
◎

Continuous Quality

Proactive monitoring and auto-fix for coverage, a11y, bundle size
See all workflows
Skill Catalog

65+ skills. Lazy-loaded on demand.

Skills are passive knowledge modules. Agents load only what they need — keeping context clean and relevant.

Core
core skill-discovery knowledge-retrieval knowledge-capture data-store sequential-thinking problem-solving error-recovery repomix
Web
web-frontend web-nextjs web-api-routes web-auth web-i18n web-testing web-modules web-prototype web-rag web-ui-lib web-a11y
iOS
ios-development ios-ui-lib ios-rag ios-a11y
Android
android-development android-ui-lib android-a11y
Backend
backend-javaee backend-databases infra-docker infra-cloud
Design System
figma design-tokens ui-lib-dev
Accessibility
a11y web-a11y ios-a11y android-a11y
Kit Authoring
kit kit-agents kit-agent-development kit-skill-development kit-hooks kit-cli kit-verify
Knowledge System

Five levels of retrieval. Zero hallucination.

Agents search internal sources first, cascade outward only when needed, and stop the moment they find sufficient context. Every answer is attributed.

Three Knowledge Tiers

◈

Procedural

How to do things — methodology, pipelines, decision frameworks

Owner: Skills
◉

Codebase

What exists in the code — components, tokens, patterns, implementations

Owner: RAG System
◎

Project

What the team decided & learned — ADRs, findings, conventions

Owner: docs/

Rule: Each piece of knowledge lives in exactly ONE tier. Other systems reference it, never copy it.

Retrieval Chain — Search in Order, Stop When Found

L1
docs/ Index query

Decisions, conventions, findings, patterns

not found →
L2
RAG Vector search

Components, tokens, implementations

not found →
L3
Skills Skill index

Methodology, procedures, guidelines

not found →
L4
Codebase Grep + Glob

Exact matches, files RAG missed

not found →
L5
External Context7 + Web

Library APIs, latest external info

last resort
docs/ — git-tracked knowledge base
docs/
├─
ADR Decisions
ADR-0001-nextjs-app-router.md
├─
ARCH Architecture
ARCH-0001-system-overview.md
├─
PATTERN Patterns
PATTERN-0003-error-boundary.md
├─
CONV Conventions
CONV-0001-named-exports.md
├─
FEAT Features
FEAT-0002-auth-flow.md
├─
FINDING Findings
FINDING-0012-ssr-hydration.md
└─ index.json machine-readable registry
Smart routing — question type → source

"What did we decide about X?"

→ L1 docs/decisions/ skip RAG, External

"How is X implemented?"

→ L2 RAG → L4 Codebase skip Skills

"What's the process for X?"

→ L3 Skills skip RAG

"How to use library X API?"

→ L5 External skip docs/

Agents know where to look — no full-scan needed.

⇄

Cross-Source Bridging

Sources validate each other. An ADR mentions a component path → RAG confirms current state. RAG finds a recurring pattern → docs/ checks for documented convention. Code pattern without docs? Flagged for capture. Decision without code? Flagged as stale. Every answer is grounded.

Install

Up in two commands.

Install the CLI globally, then run epost-kit init in your project. Agents and skills are wired automatically.

1 Install the CLI
$ npm install -g epost-kit
2 Init your project
$ epost-kit init
3 Start working
fix the login page
your first session

> plan a notification feature

Checking git state...

Detected: multi-platform, Plan intent

Loading: plan, knowledge-retrieval

Dispatching epost-planner...


Phase 1 — Backend endpoints

- POST /notifications/subscribe

- GET /notifications/preferences


Phase 2 — iOS foreground handling

- UNUserNotificationCenter delegate

- Swift 6 actor isolation


Phase 3 — Web notification center

- NotificationCenter.tsx component

- Redux slice + SSE connection


Plan saved → plans/260312-0900-notifications/

> _

Star on GitHub

npm install -g epost-kit

◈ epost_agent_kit v2.0.0
Works with any AI coding assistant
